era: 1990-1991. Grunge Supergroup. E=7 -- tribute grief, Capitol Hill, A&M. Andrew Wood memorial. Cornell + Vedder first meeting. One album, no tour. Both futures (Soundgarden + Pearl Jam) active simultaneously.
members: Stone Gossard (guitar, Mother Love Bone / Pearl Jam)
Jeff Ament (bass, Mother Love Bone / Pearl Jam)
key_tracks: Say Hello 2 Heaven -- opens album, addressed to Andrew Wood directly, slow/heavy, E=7 anchor
Hunger Strike -- Cornell/Vedder duet; commercial breakthrough 1992 MTV; the vocal pairing
Pushin Forward Back -- McCready-Gossard guitar pairing prototype; Pearl Jam Ten sonic precursor
Four Walled World -- most formally complex composition, extended structure

theory_nodes.concept: ANDREW WOOD MEMORIAL. Temple of the Dog is a direct grief document, not a calculated supergroup project. Cornell wrote all songs in response to Wood's death (March 19, 1990, age 24). The album's authenticity is structural: the emotional content was not performed for an audience but documented from real loss.
theory_nodes.description: Grief as structural authenticity: the album's origin is its most important fact.

theory_nodes.concept: CORNELL-VEDDER FIRST MEETING. Eddie Vedder arrived from San Diego on a cassette tape (audition for forming Pearl Jam, passed through Jack Irons). Cornell heard the tape and invited Vedder to the Temple sessions. The studio was the first time Vedder met the future Pearl Jam lineup. He arrived for Temple; he stayed for Pearl Jam.
theory_nodes.description: The cassette tape that brought Vedder to Seattle: the origin of Pearl Jam's formation.

theory_nodes.concept: PEARL JAM SONIC PROTOTYPE. Gossard-McCready guitar pairing (bluesy leads over dense chord voicings), Jeff Ament bass, Matt Cameron drums -- the personnel and sonic template for Ten (1991) appears first on Temple of the Dog (1991). Same studio (London Bridge), same producer (Rick Parashar). The Two records are sonic siblings.
theory_nodes.description: Temple of the Dog as Pearl Jam Ten's sonic prototype: same room, same producer, same personnel minus Vedder-on-lead.

theory_nodes.concept: DELAYED COMMERCIAL RECOGNITION. Album released April 16, 1991. Sold modestly. Commercial acceleration in 1992 as Pearl Jam became globally famous. 'Hunger Strike' heavy MTV rotation 1992. Platinum certification 1993. The delayed recognition pattern: memorial records are often not commercially immediate.
theory_nodes.description: Delayed recognition as pattern for memorial records: cultural impact precedes commercial.

cross_references.relationship: Cornell + Cameron are the Soundgarden contingent. Temple E=7 vs. Soundgarden E=8: grief controls the throttle. Same vocalist at different energy register.
Gossard + Ament + McCready + Vedder: Pearl Jam contingent. Temple = Ten prototype. Same studio. Same producer. Same guitarist pairing.
Andrew Wood's band. Apple (1990) as the release that preceded the loss that catalyzed Temple. Mother Love Bone will receive a dedicated degree.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=8 SPS + E=7 S36. Both degree-317 events are convergence events: two species on one acoustic channel; two bands in one studio. San Juan Islands multi-species vs. Capitol Hill supergroup.
